original_query: select min(w_warehouse_name)   ,min(sm_type)   ,min(cc_name)   ,min(cs_order_number)   ,min(cs_item_sk) from    catalog_sales   ,warehouse   ,ship_mode   ,call_center   ,date_dim where     d_month_seq between 1207 and 1207 + 23 and cs_ship_date_sk   = d_date_sk and cs_warehouse_sk   = w_warehouse_sk and cs_ship_mode_sk   = sm_ship_mode_sk and cs_call_center_sk = cc_call_center_sk and cs_list_price between 114 and 143 and sm_type = 'OVERNIGHT' and cc_class = 'small' and w_gmt_offset = -5 ;
rewritten_query:
WITH filtered_warehouse AS (
    SELECT w_warehouse_sk, w_warehouse_name
    FROM warehouse
    WHERE w_gmt_offset = -5
),
filtered_call_center AS (
    SELECT cc_call_center_sk, cc_name
    FROM call_center
    WHERE cc_class = 'small'
),
filtered_date_dim AS (
    SELECT d_date_sk
    FROM date_dim
    WHERE d_month_seq BETWEEN 1207 AND 1230
)
SELECT 
    MIN(w.w_warehouse_name),
    'OVERNIGHT' AS sm_type,
    MIN(cc.cc_name),
    MIN(cs.cs_order_number),
    MIN(cs.cs_item_sk)
FROM catalog_sales cs
JOIN filtered_warehouse w ON cs.cs_warehouse_sk = w.w_warehouse_sk
JOIN ship_mode sm ON cs.cs_ship_mode_sk = sm.sm_ship_mode_sk AND sm.sm_type = 'OVERNIGHT'
JOIN filtered_call_center cc ON cs.cs_call_center_sk = cc.cc_call_center_sk
JOIN filtered_date_dim dd ON cs.cs_ship_date_sk = dd.d_date_sk
WHERE cs.cs_list_price BETWEEN 114 AND 143;

rewrite_suggestion.nave_suggestion: Replace MIN(sm_type) with the constant 'OVERNIGHT' since all rows will have that value due to the filter condition.
Move filter conditions from WHERE clause into the JOIN ON clauses to apply filters earlier in the execution process.
Use explicit JOIN syntax instead of implicit joins in the WHERE clause for better readability and potentially better optimization.
